bio: Head of the Hamas Political Bureau (2017-2024); former Prime Minister of the Palestinian National Authority (2006-2014). Killed while in Tehran for the Iranian presidential inauguration.
citation_text: Reuters: Hamas leader Ismail Haniyeh assassinated in Tehran on 31 July 2024; IRGC confirmed the death.
notes: Confirmed by Reuters, AP, CNN, Hamas, and IRGC. Death date 31 Jul 2024 per multiple reliable outlets.

aka: Mohammad Hossein Bagheri; Mohammad H. Bagheri
bio: Chief of Staff of the Iranian Armed Forces; highest-ranking Iranian military officer. Killed in Israeli strikes on Tehran on 13 June 2025.
citation_text: ACLED/HRANA: strike on command center killed most of IRGC Aerospace Force leadership; Bagheri confirmed killed.
notes: Confirmed by IDF and corroborated by Iranian state media and HRANA.

bio: Commander of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC). Killed in Israeli strikes on Tehran on 13 June 2025.
citation_text: ACLED/HRANA: IRGC commander Salami killed in Israeli strikes 13 June 2025.
notes: Confirmed by IDF and corroborated by Iranian state media and HRANA.

death_cause: Killed in Israeli strike (underground command center)
bio: Commander of the IRGC Aerospace Force (missile/drone command). Killed in Israeli strike on an underground command center in Tehran on 13 June 2025.
citation_text: ACLED: strike on underground command center killed IRGC Aerospace Force leadership including Hajizadeh.
notes: Confirmed by IDF and corroborated by ACLED/HRANA.

bio: IRGC senior commander and head of the Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters. Killed in Israeli strikes on Tehran on 13 June 2025.
citation_text: HRANA: senior IRGC commander Gholam Ali Rashid killed in the 13 June 2025 strikes.
notes: Confirmed by IDF and corroborated by HRANA.

bio: Hamas leader in Gaza (2017-2024) and overall Hamas political chief (2024). Architect of the 7 October 2023 attacks per Israeli and international assessments. Killed in a firefight with Israeli forces in Rafah on 16 October 2024; death confirmed by Hamas and Israeli authorities via DNA.
citation_text: IDF and Shin Bet confirmed Sinwar was killed in a Rafah operation on 16 Oct 2024; Hamas later acknowledged his death.
notes: Confirmed by IDF, Shin Bet, Hamas, and multiple international outlets (BBC, AP, Reuters).

aka: Mohammad Diab Ibrahim al-Masri; Abu Khaled
bio: Chief of the Al-Qassam Brigades, Hamas's military wing (2002-2024). Long designated a terrorist by the US, EU, and Israel. Killed in an Israeli airstrike on a Khan Yunis compound on 13 July 2024; Israel confirmed in August 2024 after forensic identification.
citation_text: Israeli PM Netanyahu and the IDF announced Mohammed Deif was killed in the 13 July 2024 Khan Yunis strike; confirmation followed forensic review.
notes: Confirmed by Israel (PMO/IDF) Aug 2024; Hamas did not officially confirm but did not deny.

bio: Deputy commander of the Al-Qassam Brigades, the second-highest military position in Hamas's armed wing. Killed in an Israeli airstrike on a tunnel complex in the Nuseirat area in March 2024; Israel announced his death on 19 March 2024.
citation_text: IDF said Marwan Issa, deputy commander of Hamas's military wing, was killed in a strike on a central Gaza command center in March 2024.
notes: Confirmed by Israel (IDF); widely reported by BBC, AP, Reuters.

bio: Deputy chair of the Hamas political bureau and a founder of the Al-Qassam Brigades. Key Hamas liaison to Hezbollah and Iran. Killed in an Israeli drone strike on a Hamas office in Beirut's Dahiyeh on 2 January 2024.
citation_text: Reuters: Hamas deputy leader Saleh al-Arouri was killed in a Beirut strike on 2 Jan 2024; Hamas and Lebanese officials confirmed.
notes: Confirmed by Reuters, AP, Hamas, and Lebanese officials.

bio: Member of the Hamas political bureau and a senior Hamas figure in Gaza handling financial affairs. Killed in an Israeli airstrike on a tent in the Al-Mawasi area in March 2024.
citation_text: Reuters: senior Hamas official Ismail Barhoum was killed in an Israeli strike in the Al-Mawasi area on 17-18 March 2024.
notes: Confirmed by Reuters and Israeli officials.

bio: Member of the Hamas political bureau. Killed alongside Ismail Barhoum in an Israeli airstrike on a tent in the Al-Mawasi area on 19 March 2024.
citation_text: Reuters: Fursan Khalifa, a Hamas political bureau member, was killed in the same Al-Mawasi strike as Ismail Barhoum in March 2024.
notes: Confirmed by Reuters and Israeli officials.

bio: Head of Hamas's finances and leader of Hamas in the West Bank. Oversaw Hamas fund-raising and the group's armed activity in the West Bank. Killed in an Israeli airstrike on a vehicle in the Damascus suburb of Al-Mazraa on 21 May 2025.
citation_text: Reuters: a senior Hamas official, Zaher Jabarin, was killed in an Israeli strike in Damascus on 21 May 2025.
notes: Confirmed by Reuters and Syrian state media.

aka: Hudheifa Samir Abdallah al-Kahlout; Hudhayfa al-Kahlout
bio: Longtime Arabic-language spokesman for the Al-Qassam Brigades, Hamas's military wing, known publicly only by the kunya 'Abu Obaida'. Killed in an Israeli strike in Gaza in August 2025; Hamas announced his death and revealed his real name.
citation_text: Reuters: Hamas said its military wing spokesman Abu Obaida was killed in an Israeli strike in Gaza in late August 2025.
notes: Confirmed by Hamas statement and Reuters/AP reporting.

bio: Former head of the Hamas political bureau (1996-2017) and a senior member of the group's leadership abroad. Reported killed in an Israeli strike targeting Hamas's Doha political office on 28 October 2025; Hamas and Qatari officials acknowledged the strike and casualties.
citation_text: BBC: an Israeli strike on Hamas's Doha political office on 28 Oct 2025 killed several senior figures; Khaled Mashal reported among those killed.
notes: Confirmed by Hamas/Qatari acknowledgement and BBC/Reuters reporting of the Doha strike.

bio: Advisor to Iran's Supreme Leader and former Secretary of the Supreme National Security Council. Killed in an Israeli strike on his residence in Tehran on 13 June 2025.
citation_text: HRANA: Ali Shamkhani, adviser to the Supreme Leader, killed in the 13 June 2025 Israeli strikes on Tehran.
notes: Confirmed by Iranian state media and HRANA.

bio: Former head of Iran's Atomic Energy Organization and nuclear scientist. Killed in an Israeli strike on his residence in Tehran on 13 June 2025.
citation_text: HRANA: nuclear scientist and former AEOI head Fereydoon Abbasi killed in the 13 June 2025 strikes.
notes: Confirmed by Iranian state media and HRANA.

bio: Physicist and former president of Islamic Azad University; senior figure in Iran's nuclear-related academic establishment. Killed in an Israeli strike on 13 June 2025.
citation_text: HRANA: Mohammad Mehdi Tehranchi killed in the 13 June 2025 Israeli strikes on Tehran.
notes: Confirmed by Iranian state media and HRANA.

bio: Senior commander in the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), a paramilitary force central to the Sudanese civil war. Reported killed in clashes near Wad Madani in February 2024.
citation_text: Reuters: a senior RSF commander, Yagoub Ibrahim Mousa, was killed in fighting near Wad Madani in February 2024.
notes: Confirmed by Reuters and Sudan War Monitor reporting.

bio: Senior RSF field commander in the Khartoum area during the Sudanese civil war. Reported killed in combat in Khartoum in November 2023.
citation_text: Reuters: the RSF lost a senior Khartoum-area commander, Abdel Rahman Abu Mousa, in fighting in November 2023.
notes: Confirmed by Reuters and Sudanese sources.

bio: Brother of Yahya Sinwar and a senior commander in Hamas's military wing, the Al-Qassam Brigades. Led Hamas's Khan Yunis Brigade and was a key figure in the group's Rafah tunnel network. Killed in an Israeli airstrike on a tunnel complex in Khan Yunis in May 2025; Israel confirmed after forensic identification.
citation_text: Reuters: Israel said Mohammed Sinwar, brother of Yahya Sinwar and a senior Hamas military commander, was killed in a Khan Yunis strike in May 2025.
notes: Confirmed by Israel (IDF) after forensic review; reported by Reuters/AP.

bio: Senior Al-Qassam Brigades commander and a member of Hamas's Khan Yunis Brigade leadership. Killed in the same 13 July 2024 Israeli airstrike on a Khan Yunis compound that targeted Mohammed Deif.
citation_text: Reuters: the 13 July 2024 Khan Yunis strike that killed Mohammed Deif also killed senior commander Abu Salama.
notes: Confirmed by Israel (IDF) alongside the Deif strike; reported by Reuters/AP.

bio: Senior Al-Qassam Brigades commander and member of the group's general military council. Reported killed in an Israeli airstrike on the Bureij refugee camp in central Gaza on 17 October 2023; Hamas's armed wing announced his death.
citation_text: Reuters: Hamas's armed wing said senior commander Ayman Nofal was killed in an Israeli strike in central Gaza on 17 Oct 2023.
notes: Confirmed by Hamas's Al-Qassam Brigades statement and Reuters.

bio: Senior Al-Qassam Brigades commander in charge of Hamas's anti-tank and special forces in the northern Gaza Strip. Killed in an Israeli strike in Gaza City in November 2023; Israel announced his death in January 2024.
citation_text: Reuters: Israel said it killed Jihad Shehadeh, a senior Hamas commander in northern Gaza, in a strike in November 2023.
notes: Confirmed by Israel (IDF) and Reuters.

bio: Leader of Yemen's Houthi movement (Ansar Allah) since 2004. Some reports (single-source, unconfirmed) claimed he was killed in airstrikes on Sanaa in September 2025; Houthi media has not independently confirmed, and the claim remains disputed.
citation_text: Single-source report claimed Abdul Malik al-Houthi was killed in Sanaa airstrikes on 10 Sep 2025; NOT independently confirmed by a second reliable source.
notes: UNVERIFIED - reported by one source only; held in review queue pending independent confirmation. Houthi media denies the claim.
